mainEntity.name: Can I realistically manage AEO myself with software, or do I need to hire a specialist agency?

acceptedAnswer.text:
DIY AEO is realistic if you have someone comfortable with SEO, structured data, and content writing, and you’re willing to manually track snippets and AI mentions. Without that skill set, you may struggle to turn recommendations into effective changes.

Even with software, you still need humans to interpret insights, prioritize pages, and rewrite content into answer-friendly formats. The tools don’t fully replace expertise.

Key factors:
- In-house ability to implement JSON‑LD and validate schema
- Copywriting resources to create Q&A and how‑to content
- Familiarity with Search Console, analytics, and AI platforms
- Time commitment for ongoing iteration (weekly or monthly)

If you’re new to SEO, start small: pick a few pages, follow an AEO checklist, and use free tools to validate your changes. If results stall or you’re overwhelmed, that’s a sign to consider outside help.

Think of AEO as a skill you build; software turns that skill into scale, not a substitute for understanding the basics.
acceptedAnswer.description: You can manage AEO yourself if someone on your team understands SEO, schema, and content writing and can commit to ongoing iteration. AEO software helps with monitoring and recommendations, but you still need people to implement changes and interpret the data.
